articles.title: Session Timeouts: The Overlooked Accessibility Barrier In Authentication Design
articles.summary: Poorly handled session timeouts are more than a technical inconvenience. They can become serious accessibility barriers that interrupt essential online tasks, especially for people with disabilities. Here is how to implement thoughtful session management that improves usability, reduces frustration, and helps create a more accessible and respectful web.

articles.title: What improv taught me about why innovation falls out of sync
articles.summary:
When adaptation becomes uncoordinated, journeys fall out of sync — like improv comedy gone horribly wrong.
Continue reading on UX Collective »

articles.title: The UX Designer’s Nightmare: When “Production-Ready” Becomes A Design Deliverable
articles.summary: In a rush to embrace AI, the industry is redefining what it means to be a UX designer, blurring the line between design and engineering. Carrie Webster explores what’s gained, what’s lost, and why designers need to remain the guardians of the user experience.
